info.name: CCTV Cloud Storage API
info.description:
# CCTV Cloud Storage API

Multi-tenant SaaS platform for CCTV management with face recognition.

## Authentication

All protected endpoints require a **Bearer token** in the `Authorization` header:
```
Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>
```

Access tokens expire in **15 minutes**. Use `POST /api/v1/admin/auth/refresh` to obtain a new pair before expiry. Refresh tokens expire in **7 days** and are **single-use** (rotated on every refresh).

## Error Shape

All errors follow a consistent envelope:
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "ERROR_CODE",
    "message": "Human-readable description"
  }
}
```

### Error Codes
| Code | HTTP | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `INVALID_CREDENTIALS` | 401 | Wrong email or password |
| `TOKEN_INVALID` | 401 | Malformed or unverifiable token |
| `TOKEN_EXPIRED` | 401 | Token past its expiry |
| `TOKEN_REVOKED` | 401 | Refresh token already used or logged out |
| `UNAUTHORIZED` | 401 | Missing or invalid access token |
| `FORBIDDEN` | 403 | Authenticated but insufficient role |
| `VALIDATION_ERROR` | 400 | Request body/params failed schema validation |
| `NOT_FOUND` | 404 | Resource or route not found |
| `CONFLICT` | 409 | Duplicate resource |
| `RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` | 429 | Too many requests (100 req/min per IP) |
| `INTERNAL_ERROR` | 500 | Unexpected server error |

## Rate Limiting

100 requests per minute per IP. Health check allows 300/min. Response headers:
- `x-ratelimit-limit` — limit
- `x-ratelimit-remaining` — remaining in window
- `x-ratelimit-reset` — seconds until reset

## Environments

Use the companion environment files. Import the one matching your target:

| Environment | File | Base URL |
|-------------|------|----------|
| Local | `CCTV-Cloud-Storage.local.postman_environment.json` | `http://localhost:3000` |
| Staging | `CCTV-Cloud-Storage.staging.postman_environment.json` | `http://cctv-staging-alb-1931384586.eu-west-2.elb.amazonaws.com` |

### Quick Start (Staging)
1. Import this collection and the **staging** environment file into Postman.
2. Select the **CCTV Cloud Storage — Staging** environment.
3. Send **Login** (`POST /api/v1/admin/auth/login`) — tokens are stored automatically.
4. All subsequent requests use the stored `accessToken`.

**Super Admin credentials (staging):** `admin@cctv-cloud.local` / `changeme123!`

variable.description: Override with environment variable. Set to your API base URL.

KVS stream name for the camera. Set automatically by Download Camera Credentials request.

exec: // Collection-level test: validate error shape whenever status >= 400

item.name: Health
item.description: System health check. No authentication required. Verifies PostgreSQL and Redis connectivity.

request.description:
Returns the operational status of the API and its dependencies.

**No authentication required.**

### Response
```json
{
  "status": "ok",
  "db": "ok",
  "redis": "ok"
}
```

`status` is `"ok"` only when both `db` and `redis` are `"ok"`. Otherwise `"degraded"`.

### Rate Limit
300 requests per minute (higher than default).

item.name: Admin / Auth
item.description:
Super Admin authentication. These routes manage the lifecycle of RS256 JWT tokens.

- **Login** — exchange credentials for a token pair
- **Refresh** — rotate the refresh token and get a new access token (single-use, revocation via Redis)
- **Logout** — revoke the refresh token (requires a valid access token)

request.description:
Authenticates a Super Admin and returns an RS256 JWT access/refresh token pair.

**No authentication required.**

### Request Body
| Field | Type | Required | Constraints |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `email` | string | ✓ | Valid email format |
| `password` | string | ✓ | Minimum 8 characters |

### Response `200 OK`
```json
{
  "accessToken": "eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiJ9...",
  "refreshToken": "eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiJ9..."
}
```

| Token | Algorithm | Expiry | Storage recommendation |
|-------|-----------|--------|-----------------------|
| `accessToken` | RS256 | 15 min | Memory only (not localStorage) |
| `refreshToken` | RS256 | 7 days | HttpOnly cookie or secure storage |

### Error Responses
| Status | Code | Condition |
|--------|------|-----------|
| 400 | `VALIDATION_ERROR` | Invalid email format or password < 8 chars |
| 401 | `INVALID_CREDENTIALS` | Email not found or password incorrect |
| 403 | `FORBIDDEN` | Account is deactivated |

Exchanges a valid refresh token for a **new access/refresh token pair**.

**No authentication required** (the refresh token itself is the credential).

> ⚠️ **Token Rotation**: Each refresh token is **single-use**. Once used, it is revoked in Redis immediately. Reusing a consumed refresh token returns `TOKEN_REVOKED`. Store the new `refreshToken` from the response.

### Request Body
| Field | Type | Required |
|-------|------|----------|
| `refreshToken` | string | ✓ |

### Response `200 OK`
```json
{
  "accessToken": "eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiJ9...",
  "refreshToken": "eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiJ9..."
}
```

### Error Responses
| Status | Code | Condition |
|--------|------|-----------|
| 400 | `VALIDATION_ERROR` | Missing `refreshToken` field |
| 401 | `TOKEN_INVALID` | Token is malformed or signature invalid |
| 401 | `TOKEN_REVOKED` | Token has already been used or was logged out |
| 401 | `UNAUTHORIZED` | User account no longer active |

exec: pm.test('Response has new accessToken and refreshToken', () => {
pm.test('New refresh token differs from old (rotation confirmed)', () => {

request.description:
Revokes the provided refresh token, invalidating the session.

**Requires a valid Super Admin access token** (`Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>`).

After logout:
- The `refreshToken` in the request body is removed from Redis
- Any subsequent calls to `/refresh` with that token return `TOKEN_REVOKED`
- The `accessToken` remains valid until its natural 15-minute expiry (stateless — not blocklisted)

### Request Body
| Field | Type | Required |
|-------|------|----------|
| `refreshToken` | string | ✓ |

### Response `204 No Content`
Empty body on success.

### Error Responses
| Status | Code | Condition |
|--------|------|-----------|
| 400 | `VALIDATION_ERROR` | Missing `refreshToken` field |
| 401 | `UNAUTHORIZED` | Missing or expired access token |

exec: console.warn('accessToken is not set — run Login first.');
console.warn('refreshToken is not set — run Login first.');
console.log('Tokens cleared from collection variables after logout.');

item.name: Admin / Organizations
item.description: Super Admin endpoints for managing organisations. All routes require a valid Super Admin access token.

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of all organisations.

### Query Parameters
| Param | Type | Default | Max |
|-------|------|---------|-----|
| `page` | integer | 1 | — |
| `limit` | integer | 20 | 100 |

### Response `200 OK`
```json
{
  "data": [ ... ],
  "pagination": { "page": 1, "limit": 20, "total": 5 }
}
```

exec: console.warn('accessToken not set — run Login first.');
pm.test('Response has data array and pagination', () => {

request.description:
Creates a new organisation along with an `org_admin` user in a single transaction. If the user INSERT fails (e.g. duplicate email), the entire transaction rolls back.

### Request Body
| Field | Type | Required | Constraints |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `name` | string | ✓ | 1–255 chars |
| `slug` | string | | `[a-z0-9-]+`, max 100 chars, unique. Optional — auto-generated from org name if not provided. |
| `adminEmail` | string | ✓ | Valid email, unique |
| `adminPassword` | string | ✓ | Min 8 chars |

### Response `201 Created`
Returns the created organisation object (no user details).

### Error Responses
| Status | Code | Condition |
|--------|------|-----------|
| 400 | `VALIDATION_ERROR` | Invalid slug format, missing fields |
| 409 | `CONFLICT` | Slug or admin email already taken |

Fetches a single organisation by UUID.

### Path Parameters
| Param | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `orgId` | UUID | Organisation ID |

### Error Responses
| Status | Code | Condition |
|--------|------|-----------|
| 400 | `VALIDATION_ERROR` | `orgId` is not a valid UUID |
| 404 | `NOT_FOUND` | Organisation does not exist |

Partially updates an organisation. At least one field must be provided.

### Request Body (all optional, min 1)
| Field | Type | Constraints |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `name` | string | 1–255 chars |
| `slug` | string | `[a-z0-9-]+`, max 100, unique |
| `is_active` | boolean | — |

### Error Responses
| Status | Code | Condition |
|--------|------|-----------|
| 400 | `VALIDATION_ERROR` | Empty body or invalid field values |
| 404 | `NOT_FOUND` | Organisation does not exist |
| 409 | `CONFLICT` | Slug already taken by another org |

Soft-deletes an organisation by setting `is_active = false`. All users in the organisation are also deactivated in the same transaction.

### Response `204 No Content`
Empty body on success.

### Error Responses
| Status | Code | Condition |
|--------|------|-----------|
| 404 | `NOT_FOUND` | Organisation does not exist |

item.name: Admin / Users
item.description: Super Admin endpoints for managing users across all organisations. All routes require a valid Super Admin access token.

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of users. Optionally filter by `orgId`.

`password_hash` is never included in any user response.

### Query Parameters
| Param | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `page` | integer | 1 | Page number |
| `limit` | integer | 20 | Max 100 |
| `orgId` | UUID | — | Filter to one org |

exec: pm.test('Response has data array and pagination', () => {

request.description:
Fetches a single user by UUID. `password_hash` is never returned.

### Error Responses
| Status | Code | Condition |
|--------|------|-----------|
| 400 | `VALIDATION_ERROR` | `userId` is not a valid UUID |
| 404 | `NOT_FOUND` | User does not exist |

Activates or deactivates a user account.

### Request Body
| Field | Type | Required |
|-------|------|----------|
| `is_active` | boolean | ✓ |

### Error Responses
| Status | Code | Condition |
|--------|------|-----------|
| 400 | `VALIDATION_ERROR` | Missing or invalid `is_active` |
| 404 | `NOT_FOUND` | User does not exist |

Permanently deletes a user. `super_admin` users cannot be deleted.

### Response `204 No Content`
Empty body on success.

### Error Responses
| Status | Code | Condition |
|--------|------|-----------|
| 403 | `FORBIDDEN` | Target user is a `super_admin` |
| 404 | `NOT_FOUND` | User does not exist |

item.name: Auth
item.description:
Org user authentication endpoints. Supports org_admin and viewer roles.

Use **Login** to obtain tokens, then subsequent requests auto-use `orgAccessToken`.

request.description:
Authenticates an org user (org_admin or viewer) and returns an RS256 JWT access/refresh token pair.

**No authentication required.**

### Request Body
| Field | Type | Required | Constraints |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `email` | string | ✓ | Valid email format |
| `password` | string | ✓ | Minimum 8 characters |

### Response `200 OK`
```json
{
  "accessToken": "eyJ...",
  "refreshToken": "eyJ..."
}
```

### Error Responses
| Status | Code | Condition |
|--------|------|-----------|
| 400 | `VALIDATION_ERROR` | Invalid email format or password < 8 chars |
| 401 | `INVALID_CREDENTIALS` | Email not found or password incorrect |
| 403 | `FORBIDDEN` | Account or organization is deactivated |

exec: // Store org tokens for subsequent requests
console.log('Org tokens stored in collection variables.');

request.description:
Exchanges a valid refresh token for a new access/refresh token pair. The old refresh token is revoked (rotation).

**No authentication required** — the refresh token is the credential.

### Request Body
| Field | Type | Required |
|-------|------|----------|
| `refreshToken` | string | ✓ |

### Response `200 OK`
```json
{
  "accessToken": "eyJ...",
  "refreshToken": "eyJ..."
}
```

Revokes the provided refresh token. Requires a valid org user access token.

### Request Body
| Field | Type | Required |
|-------|------|----------|
| `refreshToken` | string | ✓ |

### Response `204 No Content`
Empty body.

Changes the authenticated org user's password.

### Request Body
| Field | Type | Required | Constraints |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `currentPassword` | string | ✓ | Minimum 8 characters |
| `newPassword` | string | ✓ | Minimum 8 characters |

### Response `200 OK`
```json
{
  "message": "Password changed successfully"
}
```

item.name: Org / Users
item.description: Org admin user management. Requires `org_admin` role. All queries are scoped to the authenticated user's organization.

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of users in the authenticated admin's organization.

### Query Parameters
| Param | Type | Default | Max |
|-------|------|---------|-----|
| `page` | integer | 1 | — |
| `limit` | integer | 20 | 100 |

### Response `200 OK`
```json
{
  "data": [{ "id": "...", "email": "...", "role": "viewer", "org_id": "...", "is_active": true }],
  "pagination": { "page": 1, "limit": 20, "total": 5 }
}
```

Creates a new viewer user in the authenticated admin's organization.

### Request Body
| Field | Type | Required | Constraints |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `email` | string | ✓ | Valid email, unique |
| `password` | string | ✓ | Minimum 8 characters |

### Response `201 Created`
```json
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "email": "viewer@example.com",
  "role": "viewer",
  "org_id": "uuid",
  "is_active": true,
  "created_at": "...",
  "updated_at": "..."
}
```

exec: pm.test('Response has user with viewer role', () => {
// Store user id for subsequent requests

request.description:
Returns a single user by ID. Returns 403 if the user belongs to a different organization (cross-org isolation).

### Response `200 OK`
```json
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "email": "viewer@example.com",
  "role": "viewer",
  "org_id": "uuid",
  "is_active": true,
  "created_at": "...",
  "updated_at": "..."
}
```

Updates a user in the authenticated admin's organization. Currently supports toggling `is_active`.

### Request Body
| Field | Type | Required |
|-------|------|----------|
| `is_active` | boolean | ✓ |

### Response `200 OK`
Returns the updated user object.

Deletes a viewer user from the authenticated admin's organization. Cannot delete org_admin users.

### Response `204 No Content`
Empty body.

### Error Responses
| Status | Code | Condition |
|--------|------|-----------|
| 403 | `FORBIDDEN` | Attempting to delete an org_admin |
| 403 | `FORBIDDEN` | User belongs to a different organization |
| 404 | `NOT_FOUND` | User not found |

item.name: ⚙️ Workflow — Full Auth Lifecycle
item.description:
Run these requests **in order** to walk through the complete token lifecycle:

1. **Login** → stores `accessToken` + `refreshToken`
2. **Refresh Tokens** → rotates both tokens
3. **Logout** → revokes the session
4. **[Verify] Refresh after logout** → confirms `TOKEN_REVOKED`

This folder is intended for exploratory/manual testing and onboarding. Use the Runner to execute all four in sequence.

request.description: Step 1 of the full auth lifecycle. Stores tokens automatically.

exec: // Stash original refresh for the revocation test at step 4

request.description: Step 2 of the full auth lifecycle. Rotates both tokens.
Step 3 of the full auth lifecycle. Revokes the current refresh token.

exec: // Stash the revoked token for step 4 verification

request.description: Step 4 of the full auth lifecycle. Confirms the refresh token was revoked on logout.

exec: console.log('[Step 4] Revocation confirmed — full lifecycle test complete.');

item.name: Cameras
item.description: Camera management endpoints. Org Admins can create, update, and delete cameras. All org users (including viewers) can list and view cameras.

request.description:
Create a new camera and provision a KVS stream + IoT Thing.

**Auth:** `requireOrgAdmin`

### Request Body
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `name` | string | Yes | Camera name (1-255 chars) |
| `slug` | string | No | URL-friendly identifier. Lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens (`[a-z0-9-]+`). Auto-generated as `cam1`, `cam2`, etc. if not provided. Must be unique within the org. |
| `location` | string | No | Physical location |
| `timezone` | string | No | IANA timezone (default: UTC) |
| `rtsp_url` | string | No | RTSP stream URL (encrypted at rest via KMS) |

### Response
Camera status starts as `provisioning`. It transitions to `online` when the device starts streaming (via internal webhook).

The `kvs_stream_name` is built as `{orgSlug}-{cameraSlug}` (e.g. `acme-corp-front-door` or `acme-corp-cam1`).

List all active cameras for the authenticated user's organization.

**Auth:** `requireUser` (viewers and org admins)

**Caching:** Redis-cached with 2-min TTL. Cache invalidated on create/update/delete.

### Query Parameters
| Param | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `page` | integer | 1 | Page number |
| `limit` | integer | 20 | Items per page (max 100) |

Get a single camera by ID. Returns decrypted RTSP URL if set.

**Auth:** `requireUser` (viewers and org admins)

Returns 403 if the camera belongs to a different organization.
Download IoT credentials for a camera. One-time download — returns 409 if already issued.

Update camera properties (name, location, timezone, rtsp_url).

**Auth:** `requireOrgAdmin`

At least one field must be provided. RTSP URL is re-encrypted on update.

Deactivate a camera and clean up all AWS resources.

**Auth:** `requireOrgAdmin`

Performs full cleanup: deactivates IoT certificate, detaches policy, deletes certificate, deletes IoT Thing, deletes KVS stream. Soft-deletes in DB (sets `is_active = false`, `status = inactive`).

item.name: Camera Assignments
item.description: Camera-viewer assignment management. Org Admins can assign/unassign viewers to cameras from both camera-centric and user-centric perspectives.

request.description:
Assign one or more viewer users to a camera.

**Auth:** `requireOrgAdmin`

### Request Body
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `user_ids` | uuid[] | Yes | Array of viewer user IDs to assign |

Replace all viewer assignments for a camera. Removes existing assignments and sets the provided list.

**Auth:** `requireOrgAdmin`

### Request Body
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `user_ids` | uuid[] | Yes | Array of viewer user IDs (replaces all existing) |

Remove one or more viewer assignments from a camera.

**Auth:** `requireOrgAdmin`

### Request Body
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `user_ids` | uuid[] | Yes | Array of viewer user IDs to remove |

Assign one or more cameras to a viewer user.

**Auth:** `requireOrgAdmin`

### Request Body
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `camera_ids` | uuid[] | Yes | Array of camera IDs to assign |

Replace all camera assignments for a viewer user. Removes existing assignments and sets the provided list.

**Auth:** `requireOrgAdmin`

### Request Body
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `camera_ids` | uuid[] | Yes | Array of camera IDs (replaces all existing) |

Remove one or more camera assignments from a viewer user.

**Auth:** `requireOrgAdmin`

### Request Body
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `camera_ids` | uuid[] | Yes | Array of camera IDs to remove |

item.name: Admin / Cameras
item.description: Super Admin camera management. View all cameras across all organizations.

request.description:
List all cameras across all organizations (super admin only).

**Auth:** `requireSuperAdmin`

### Query Parameters
| Param | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `page` | integer | 1 | Page number |
| `limit` | integer | 20 | Items per page (max 100) |
| `org_id` | uuid | — | Optional org filter |

item.name: Internal
item.description: Internal webhook endpoints. Authenticated via `x-internal-secret` header (not JWT). Used by Lambda functions and other internal services.

header.description: Shared secret for internal service auth

request.description:
Update the online/offline status of a camera via its KVS stream name.

**Auth:** `x-internal-secret` header (shared secret, NOT JWT)

Called by Lambda when KVS stream connectivity changes.

### Request Body
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `kvs_stream_name` | string | Yes | KVS stream name (`{orgId}-{cameraId}`) |
| `status` | string | Yes | `online` or `offline` |
